title: Maprounea gracilis
intro: Maprounea gracilis is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as a medicinal tree native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Maprounea gracilis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Maprounea gracilis is classified within the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family, which comprises a diverse range of plant species. This family is further divided into 275 genera. The Euphorbiaceae family is a member of the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants.
